title: Acknowledgements, Delivery Tags, Redelivery, and Effect Ownership · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for acknowledgements, delivery tags, redelivery, and effect ownership and requires the smallest defensible delivery ownership and acknowledgement decision table with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for delivery ownership and acknowledgement decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.
Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use.
Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff
steps.instruction:

content.text: A quiz messaging team handles case 9505 in a canary consumer reading old and new envelope versions. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce same-generation, exactly-one terminal acknowledgement after durable effect; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.
